title: parodiophyllochloa ovulifera
intro: Parodiophyllochloa ovulifera, also known as the ovule grass, is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Parodiophyllochloa ovulifera include treating fever, wounds, and digestive issues due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. However, I couldn't find information on specific herbal preparations for this plant, and it is not widely studied or used in modern medicine.
